params.caller: The address of the account which is calling the subject protected by the policy engine.
params.context: Additional information or authorization to perform the operation.
params.parameters: The parameters to use for running the policy.
params.selector: The selector of the method being called on the protected contract.
params.subject: The address of the contract which is being protected by the policy engine.

params.caller: The address of the account which is calling the subject protected by the policy engine.
params.context: Additional information or authorization to perform the operation.
params.parameters: The parameters to use for running the policy.
params.selector: The selector of the method being called on the protected contract.
params.subject: The address of the contract which is being protected by the policy engine.

returns._0: The result of running the policy.

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

returns._0: A string representing the type and version of the policy.

onInstall(bytes4).notice: Hook called upon installation of the policy.

onUninstall(bytes4).notice: Hook called upon uninstallation of the policy.

postRun(address,address,bytes4,bytes[],b.notice: Runs after the policy check if the check was successful, and MAY mutate state. State mutations SHOULD consider state relative to the target, as the policy MAY be shared across multiple targets.

typeAndVersion().notice: Returns the type and version of the policy.
